description: Curated external audio corpora for realtime multi-speaker translation evaluation. This file records acquisition, license, benchmark roles, and detractor notes before any large audio is downloaded.

policy.default_storage: Downloaded corpus audio must stay under artifacts/audio_eval/external_corpora or another ignored local path.
policy.commit_rule: Commit manifests, acquisition notes, hashes, and tiny derived annotations only; do not commit third-party audio unless the source explicitly permits redistribution and the repo has attribution files.
policy.license_rule: Use status approved_candidate only when the official source gives clear public benchmark terms. Use review_required for registration, non-commercial, per-clip, YouTube-derived, or custom terms.
policy.privacy_rule: Do not use crowd or meeting data for product demos unless the source terms and consent posture allow that use. Keep public-figure and meeting datasets in benchmark-only mode until reviewed.

role_definitions.real_room_meeting: Real or replayed meeting-room speech with multiple speakers and room acoustics.
role_definitions.crowd_noise: Background crowd, applause, chatter, or public-space ambience for capture stress tests.
role_definitions.diarization: Speaker turn/segment annotations are available or can be derived from official labels.
role_definitions.speech_separation: Mixtures or source references are useful for overlap/separation/TSE evaluation.
role_definitions.multilingual_asr: Speech and text labels support language ID, ASR, or translation evaluation.
role_definitions.source_suppression: Useful for playback loopback, acoustic echo, noise suppression, or residual-source evaluation.
role_definitions.voice_reference: Useful for consent-aware voice similarity or TTS reference experiments.

corpora.name: NOTSOFAR-1 Recorded Meeting Dataset
corpora.size_notes: 237 recorded meetings, about 6 minutes each, in 30 conference rooms with 4-8 attendees and 35 unique speakers.
corpora.access: Official challenge repo and Hugging Face subsets; use official download helper and cache under artifacts.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Create a tiny rolling-PCM diarization fixture from one development meeting with overlap and transient noise.
corpora.why_it_matters: Closest first public match for the app's noisy distant-meeting capture path.
corpora.detractor_note: English meeting transcription data is still not multilingual live translation, and public benchmark splits must not be mixed across train/dev/test.

sources.evidence: Official repo describes recorded meetings, download helpers, and CC-BY-4.0 data license.
NOTSOFAR-1 challenge paper describes distant diarization and ASR benchmark data.

corpora.name: AMI Meeting Corpus
corpora.size_notes: About 100 hours of meetings with synchronized close-talk and far-field microphones plus transcription and annotations.
corpora.access: Official registration/download or Hugging Face mirrors; verify attribution and subset naming.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Use SDM/MDM-style distant mic audio for diarization label stability and overlap recall.
corpora.why_it_matters: Canonical meeting corpus with rich annotations and multiple microphone perspectives.
corpora.detractor_note: Mostly structured English meetings, not free-form crowds; only publicly released signals/annotations should be used.

sources.evidence: Official AMI page describes 100h meetings, synchronized microphones, annotations, and CC-BY-4.0 public release.

corpora.name: VoxConverse
corpora.size_notes: Over 50 hours of multispeaker speech clips extracted from public videos.
corpora.access: Official VoxConverse download or diarizers-community mirror; retain benchmark-only posture until review.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Use as a wild diarization stress set after AMI/NOTSOFAR pass.
corpora.why_it_matters: More in-the-wild overlap, crowd, music, and broadcast variation than clean meeting corpora.
corpora.detractor_note: YouTube-derived public-figure material is useful for benchmark-only evaluation but not a consent-safe voice-cloning or product-demo source.

sources.evidence: Official repo states research-purpose CC-BY-4.0 availability and diarization-in-the-wild publication.
Interspeech 2020 VoxConverse paper.

corpora.name: LibriCSS
corpora.size_notes: Continuous far-field replay recordings derived from LibriSpeech with varying overlap ratios.
corpora.access: Official LibriCSS repository/scripts; cache under artifacts.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Run rolling diarization and future separation/TSE gates on 10/20/30/40 percent overlap mini-sessions.
corpora.why_it_matters: Directly targets continuous speech separation with partially overlapped long-form audio.
corpora.detractor_note: It is replayed LibriSpeech in a room, not spontaneous live crowd interaction.

sources.evidence: ICASSP 2020 page describes LibriCSS as far-field replayed LibriSpeech with public dataset/evaluation scripts.
Official evaluation pipeline and download guidance.

corpora.name: LibriMix / Libri2Mix / Libri3Mix
corpora.size_notes: Two- and three-speaker mixtures with clean and noisy variants; full generation is hundreds of GB.
corpora.access: Official generation scripts; generate only tiny subsets for local smoke checks.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Create a 2-speaker and 3-speaker overlap stress fixture for separation and diarization regression.
corpora.why_it_matters: Best lightweight path for controlled overlap and source-reference scoring.
corpora.detractor_note: Synthetic mixtures are not room crowds and can overstate separation quality.

sources.evidence: Official repo describes LibriMix as an open source dataset for speech separation with 2/3 speaker generation.
LibriMix paper describes open-source generalizable speech separation data.

corpora.name: FSD50K / Freesound crowd ambience
corpora.size_notes: 51,197 human-labeled Freesound clips across 200 AudioSet classes; includes crowd/applause/chatter-like sound events through ontology labels.
corpora.access: Use official Zenodo/Freesound metadata; filter to CC0 or CC-BY clips and keep attribution.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Mix CC0/CC-BY crowd ambience under AMI/NOTSOFAR/LibriCSS snippets for noise robustness gates.
corpora.why_it_matters: Gives real crowd ambience without pretending it is diarized speech.
corpora.detractor_note: Use as benchmark-only ambience until per-clip licenses and attribution are checked; not suitable as speaker-ground-truth data.

sources.evidence: Freesound Labs lists FSD50K as an open human-labeled sound-event dataset with Zenodo download.
FSD50K paper describes open human-labeled sound events and AudioSet ontology labels.

corpora.name: MUSAN
corpora.size_notes: 11GB corpus of music, speech, and noise recordings.
corpora.access: OpenSLR SLR17 download; use noise subset for baseline augmentation.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Add a deterministic background-noise layer to existing real-speech fixtures.
corpora.why_it_matters: Simple, well-known noise corpus for capture hygiene and false-positive tests.
corpora.detractor_note: General noise is not the same as dense live crowd speech.

sources.evidence: OpenSLR lists MUSAN as music/speech/noise under CC-BY-4.0.
MUSAN corpus paper describing the music, speech, and noise corpus.

corpora.name: Mozilla Common Voice
corpora.size_notes: Large community speech corpus with many language releases; current Data Collective listing includes CC0 datasets.
corpora.access: Mozilla Data Collective/Common Voice downloads; keep source language split and datasheet.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Sample non-English clips to build language-ID and same-volume translation fixtures.
corpora.why_it_matters: Best permissive multilingual speech source for early LID/ASR smoke tests.
corpora.detractor_note: Mostly prompted speech, not overlapping crowds; voice-cloning usage still needs explicit consent policy beyond dataset license.

sources.evidence: Mozilla Data Collective lists Common Voice datasets and CC0 licensing.

corpora.name: FLEURS
corpora.size_notes: N-way parallel read-speech dataset in 102 languages, about 12 hours per language.
corpora.access: Hugging Face dataset with language configs; use tiny per-language sampled fixtures.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Test language ID and ASR/translation routing across short windows.
corpora.why_it_matters: Covers language breadth that meeting/crowd corpora do not.
corpora.detractor_note: Read-speech benchmark can hide failures on noisy spontaneous room audio.

sources.evidence: Dataset card describes 102-language FLEURS corpus and CC-BY licensing.
FLEURS paper describing the 102-language speech benchmark.

corpora.name: AliMeeting / M2MeT
corpora.size_notes: About 120 hours of Mandarin meetings with near-field headset and 8-channel far-field microphone array data.
corpora.access: Official GitHub/OpenSLR 119/Codalab path; verify dataset terms before automated download.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Use only after terms review for Mandarin overlap and DiariST-style Chinese-to-English evaluation.
corpora.why_it_matters: Strong real meeting source for non-English diarization and translation pressure.
corpora.detractor_note: Terms and split rules need review before benchmark-only use; Mandarin meetings do not cover all multilingual/crowd conditions.

sources.evidence: Official baseline repo links AliMeeting download and M2MeT challenge papers.
M2MeT paper describes the AliMeeting corpus and challenge.

corpora.name: Ego4D Audio-Visual Diarization
corpora.size_notes: Large egocentric video/audio dataset with audio-visual conversation tasks.
corpora.access: Requires license agreement acceptance and targeted task download.
corpora.recommended_first_use: Review later for egocentric/crowd conversation failure cases, not the first benchmark pack.
corpora.why_it_matters: Could stress moving-wearer, social, and real-world audio conditions.
corpora.detractor_note: Custom license, privacy posture, and video modality make it too heavy for the first benchmark-only audio gate.

sources.evidence: Official docs describe Ego4D data, audio availability, and license acceptance requirement.
Audio-visual diarization benchmark repository.

deferred_or_rejected.reason: Useful ontology and precomputed features, but raw YouTube audio availability/licensing is unstable. Prefer FSD50K/Freesound CC-filtered clips for committed crowd-noise acquisition.
Common diarization benchmark but LDC licensing makes it unsuitable as a default free/disposable fixture.
Do not use unless each recording has explicit license, attribution, consent posture, and stable download metadata.
